messages.content:
Explain how the attack is initiated by an adversary.

A firmware update vulnerability exists in the login.cgi functionality of Wavlink AC3000 M33A8.V5030.210505. A specially crafted HTTP request can lead to arbitrary firmware update. An attacker can send an unauthenticated message to trigger this vulnerability.

An adversary initiates the attack by sending a specially crafted HTTP request to the login.cgi endpoint of the device, which does not require authentication, thereby triggering an arbitrary firmware update.
